title: The Enumerable Module 🧩
description: Discover the most powerful Mixin in the entire Ruby language.

slides.title: The Magic Collection 🎩
slides.content:
Arrays and Hashes are completely different classes. Yet, they both neatly gracefully share the exact same `.each` method.

How is that mathematically possible without inheriting from a common Parent class?

slides.title: The Enumerable Mixin 🧩
slides.content:
Because Ruby relies heavily on Mixins!

Both the Array Class and the Hash Class implicitly heavily `include` a massive, world-famous Ruby module named **Enumerable**.

Enumerable contains dozens of incredibly powerful, incredibly fast iteration methods that instantly apply to both collections.

slides.title: Checking Inclusion
slides.content: Run this! You can actually explicitly ask a Class what modules it has seamlessly absorbed using `.included_modules`.
slides.codeSnippet:
puts "Does Array include Enumerable?"
puts Array.include?(Enumerable)

puts "\nDoes Hash include Enumerable?"
puts Hash.include?(Enumerable)

quizData.question: How do Arrays and Hashes magically neatly share the exact identical looping methods despite being totally unrelated code?

options.text: They both inherit from the Looper parent class.
They both securely `include` the massive `Enumerable` Module.

quizData.explanation: The Enumerable module is the beating heart of Ruby data manipulation. It is the perfect supreme example of a Mixin.

slides.title: The Requirement 🤝
slides.content:
If you build your entirely own Custom Class (like `class Playlist`), you can easily `include Enumerable` yourself!

However, the Enumerable module strictly rigorously demands one thing: Your class MUST explicitly define an `each` method that mathematically yields its items. The module uses your `each` method as its engine to power all of its 50+ magical methods!
